directions.name: Revisit the 128x128x32 hot-band branch on top of the current grouped-order plus launch-bounds base
directions.hypothesis: Earlier 128x128x32 attempts were measured before the grouped-order and compiler-clue improvements that define the current best branch. Those changes materially altered the residency regime: the current winner already runs in a 2-CTA register-limited mode, which narrows the occupancy disadvantage that previously hurt K32. That makes it worth re-testing the 128x128x32 branch with the same grouped CTA order and the same mild `launch_bounds(128, 2)` guidance, because K32 still offers the best chance to reduce mainloop control overhead if its old occupancy penalty is no longer the dominant cost.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Stage-depth / control-overhead tradeoff under the new best branch conditions, not under the older pre-grouped baseline.
directions.risk: Shared footprint is still higher than K16, so K32 may remain slower even in the new regime. This is a deliberate re-test, not a guaranteed promotion.
metrics_to_recheck: correctness pass rate on all benchmark cases
directions.stop_condition: Stop if K32 remains slower than the current best grouped K16 branch after inheriting the same grouped-order and launch-bounds conditions.

directions.name: Keep the current best K16 branch fixed and revisit tiny barrier-side cleanup only if the K32 re-test fails again
directions.hypothesis: If K32 still loses under the new conditions, the safest path is to stay with the grouped K16 winner and look for crumbs in barrier cleanup rather than keep re-opening large structural branches.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Residual barrier overhead in the grouped-order plus launch-bounds K16 winner.
directions.risk: Very limited headroom remains on that axis.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the current best branch already minimizes barrier impact sufficiently.

directions.name: Freeze the current best hot-band branch and look at a small secondary-region optimization only after the K32 re-test result is known
directions.hypothesis: If both K16 and K32 hot-band options are well understood, the next orthogonal area is the smaller non-hot-band remainder. That should stay behind the K32 re-test because the hot band still dominates total runtime.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Secondary-region overhead outside the main hot band.
directions.risk: Limited ceiling because those regions are much smaller than the hot band.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the hot-band choice still dominates the overall result.

notes: Human-idea reflection for round 29: Stage is promoted again, but specifically as a re-evaluation of the K32 branch under the new grouped-order plus compiler-guided base. The earlier K32 rejection remains valid for the old regime, but the current best branch changed the occupancy/codegen picture enough that the comparison should be refreshed once. Register Reuse and L2 Cache remain accepted because the current best branch already depends on both. Tiling 256x128, aggressive launch-bounds, and the peeled schedule remain rejected.
